skill.name: product-naming
skill.description: 产品命名协作流程。当用户想给产品/项目/模块起名字时使用。通过"灵魂挖掘 → 约束提取 → 路线发散 → 方向选择 → 竞品验证 → 最终确认"的结构化流程，从模糊想法产出有品牌生命力的名字，避免拍脑袋起名。
skill.summary: Structured product naming workflow that guides teams from fuzzy ideas to brand-ready names through collaborative iteration.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: All static scanner findings are false positives. The detected "Ruby/shell backtick execution" patterns are markdown code block examples showing output format, not actual shell commands. The "weak cryptographic algorithm" and "high file entropy" alerts stem from YAML frontmatter misinterpreted by the scanner. This is a pure text-based prompting skill with no executable code, network calls, or file system operations.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Block Examples Misidentified as Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged lines 47, 55, 68, 84, 121, 127, 135 as "Ruby/shell backtick execution". These are markdown code blocks containing example OUTPUT FORMATS showing how the skill should respond to users. No actual shell execution occurs - these are text examples within triple backticks in a documentation file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are clearly marked markdown code blocks (```) containing example responses showing output format. No shell commands are executed - the backticks are literal markdown syntax, not Ruby/shell backtick operators.

low_findings.title: False Positive: YAML Frontmatter Misidentified as Cryptographic Content
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged line 3 for "weak cryptographic algorithm". Line 3 contains YAML frontmatter (name: product-naming). No cryptographic algorithms are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: YAML frontmatter with skill metadata. No encryption, hashing, or cryptographic operations exist in this file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High File Entropy from YAML and Chinese Characters
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged high entropy (7.50 bits). This is normal for YAML frontmatter containing diverse characters and Chinese text in the content body.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: High entropy stems from Chinese characters and YAML syntax. This is a plain text markdown file, not encrypted or binary content.

content.user_title: Generate Brand-Ready Product Names with AI Collaboration
content.value_statement: Naming a product is hard. This skill transforms vague ideas into brand-ready names through a structured 6-step collaboration process that uncovers product essence before suggesting options.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through structured discovery questions to understand product essence before suggesting names
Identifies naming constraints and tensions between user requirements
Generates 2-4 naming路线 (routes/directions) with example names for user selection
Conducts competitor naming research to inform strategy
Provides final naming recommendations with decision rationale
Produces complete naming documentation including English name, Chinese name, and slogan
limitations: Does not register or check domain/name availability
Cannot verify trademark conflicts - users must do their own legal checks
Names are suggestions based on provided context - final choice remains with user
Requires meaningful user input to produce quality results

use_cases.title: Startup Founders Naming New Products
use_cases.description: Founders who have a product concept but struggle to find a memorable name. The workflow helps articulate product essence before brainstorming, resulting in names that reflect brand identity.

use_cases.title: Development Teams Naming Projects or Tools
use_cases.description: Engineering teams needing names for internal tools, open source projects, or developer products. The structured approach produces professional names aligned with technical identity.

use_cases.title: Product Managers Renaming Existing Products
use_cases.description: PMs repositioning a product who need a name refresh. The competitor analysis step helps differentiate from existing market options.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Name Request
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to name my [product type]. It's for [target user] and helps them [key benefit].
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic request with minimal context - skill will ask follow-up questions

prompt_templates.title: Named Product with Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm building a [description]. I want the name to feel [adjective]. My top competitor is [name]. Can you help me find a name?
prompt_templates.scenario: User has some brand direction and competitive context

prompt_templates.title: Complete Brief with Constraints
prompt_templates.prompt: Product: [description]. Target: [audience]. Brand feel: [adjectives]. Must: [hard requirements]. Avoid: [forbidden words]. Competitors: [list]. Please follow your naming process.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides comprehensive brief - skill will validate and proceed

prompt_templates.title: Iterative Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: We've been working on a name but it's not quite right. Current favorite: [name]. Problem: [issue]. Can you explore alternatives from a different angle?
prompt_templates.scenario: Refining an existing direction - skill will pivot and explore new routes

output_examples.input: I want to name my team standup tool. It's for remote dev teams.
output_examples.output:
📌 Product Name: Ping
📌 Slogan: Light touch, standup done
📌 Naming Strategy: Single English name
📌 Decision Basis:
- User Profile: Remote dev teams
- Product: Async standup replacement
- Constraint: Light, not corporate
- Competitor Pattern: Geekbot, Range use short English names

output_examples.input: We need a name for our AI code review plugin. It should feel smart but approachable.
output_examples.output:
Route A: Code Metaphor
Examples: Stitch, Patch, Weave
✅ Technical credibility
❌ May sound like textile industry

Route B: Conversation Terms
Examples: Chatback, ReplyAI, Echo
✅ Suggests interaction
❌ Generic AI vibes

Route C: Developer Speak
Examples: Nod, ACK, ++
✅ Developer-native
❌ Too casual for enterprise

best_practices: Answer discovery questions thoroughly - the more context you provide, the better the name recommendations
Wait for the full workflow - resist picking a name before exploring the routing options
Be honest about competitor names you want to differentiate from
anti_patterns: Asking for a list of names immediately without explaining the product
Only evaluating names on "sounds good" rather than brand strategy fit
Ignoring naming constraints or competitor research

faq.question: Does this skill check if domain names are available?
faq.answer: No. This skill focuses on the creative and strategic aspects of naming. You should check domain availability separately after selecting a name.

faq.question: Can it generate names in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow supports Chinese, English, or hybrid naming strategies based on your target market and user preferences.

faq.question: How long does the naming process take?
faq.answer: Plan for a back-and-forth conversation. Plan for 10-20 minutes to work through the full 6-step process with thoughtful responses.

faq.question: What if I don't like any of the suggested names?
faq.answer: The workflow is iterative. You can reject all options and provide new direction. The skill will explore alternative routes based on your feedback.

faq.question: Does this check for trademark conflicts?
faq.answer: No. While competitor research is part of the workflow, you must conduct your own trademark search before committing to a name.

faq.question: Can I use this for renaming an existing product?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow works for both new products and rebranding efforts. Include information about why you're renaming for better guidance.
